raw_consumer_statement:
This letter serves as a formal dispute against Wayfair and Citibank regarding the {$110.00} in shipping charges deducted from my recent Wayfair returns. This amount represents the difference between my original purchases ( {$760.00} ) and the refund amount received ( {$640.00} ). 

On XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, I made purchases on Wayfair totaling {$760.00}, consisting of XXXX charges of {$470.00} and {$280.00} respectively. After receiving and testing the products, I determined they did not meet my requirements and initiated a return within Wayfair 's 30-day return policy. 

During the return process, shipping charges were applied that had not been disclosed at the time of purchase. My previous experiences with Wayfair returns did not include such charges, indicating a policy change that was not communicated during checkout. I would simply not purchase from Wayfair had there been such a significant shipping return fee. 

Following the return of all items, I received a total refund of {$640.00} across XXXX separate refund transactions. The {$110.00} difference between amount paid and amount refunded consists of shipping charges deducted without prior notification. 

I contacted Wayfair customer service and was directed to XXXX. This account is notorious for responding only after the return window had closed. This pattern appears consistent with reports from other customers. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I initiated a dispute with Citibank. The claim was initially misunderstood as a missing refund issue and was closed on XX/XX/XXXX. I reopened the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, clarifying that I was specifically disputing the shipping charges deduction. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received notice that Wayfair had refused to issue credits and that Citibank could not further assist with this dispute without providing any additional context. 

This constitutes an unfair business practice based on : 1. Lack of disclosure regarding return shipping charges at checkout 2. Undisclosed change from previous Wayfair return policies 3. Ineffective customer service response channel I am requesting a credit of {$110.00}, representing the shipping charges deducted from my refund. This request is based on the lack of clear disclosure regarding these charges at the time of purchase.
